findings.title: CreateCommentDto.content and UpdateCommentDto.content lack @MaxLength — unbounded free-text field
findings.description: The `content` field in both CreateCommentDto and UpdateCommentDto is validated with @IsString() and @IsNotEmpty() but has no @MaxLength constraint. An authenticated user with `comments:create` can POST an arbitrarily large string, consuming DB storage (TEXT column is unlimited in PostgreSQL), and potentially triggering DoS-class conditions in any downstream code that copies or logs the content. The UpdateCommentDto (line 7-9 in update-comment.dto.ts) has the same issue.
findings.root_cause: Free-text fields that accept arbitrary user input were not capped at definition time.
findings.impact: Any user with comments:create (down to CONTRIBUTEUR / OBSERVATEUR who both have tasks:read and therefore can reach the comment endpoint) can store very large blobs in the database. If content is ever serialised to memory (e.g. audit log, CSV export) this may cause OOM on small instances.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MaxLength(10000)` (or an appropriate domain limit) to `content` in both CreateCommentDto and UpdateCommentDto. Example: `@MaxLength(10000, { message: 'Comment content cannot exceed 10 000 characters' })`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /comments with content longer than the limit returns HTTP 400
PATCH /comments/:id with content longer than the limit returns HTTP 400
findings.verification_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/comments -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"content":"'$(python3 -c 'print("A"*20001)')'" ,"taskId":"<uuid>"}' | jq .statusCode
findings.notes: Same pattern exists for CreateTaskDto.description (no @MaxLength), ImportTaskDto.description and .title, CreatePredefinedTaskDto.name/description/color/icon, CreateSubtaskDto.description — all lack @MaxLength. This finding focuses on the comment vector as the most reachable by low-privilege users.

findings.title: CreateTaskDto.tags field has no @IsArray / @IsString({ each }) / @MaxLength validators — arbitrary data injection
findings.description: The `tags` field is declared as `string[]` but the only decorator is `@IsOptional()`. The global ValidationPipe runs with `whitelist:true` + `forbidNonWhitelisted:true` + `transform:true`, so the field is whitelisted. However, without `@IsArray()` + `@IsString({ each: true })` + `@MaxLength(N, { each: true })` + `@ArrayMaxSize(N)`, a caller can submit an array of arbitrary objects, non-string elements, or extremely large arrays/strings. Prisma will store whatever TypeScript receives after transformation, which may include arbitrary nested data depending on how `tags` is typed in the Prisma schema.
findings.root_cause: The `tags` field was added to the DTO without completing the class-validator decorator set required for array-of-strings validation.
findings.impact: Authenticated users with `tasks:create` can inject non-string elements or very large tag arrays into the database. If tags are rendered in the UI without escaping, this is a stored XSS surface.
acceptance_criteria: POST /tasks with tags: [{evil: true}] returns HTTP 400
POST /tasks with tags containing 21 elements returns HTTP 400
findings.verification_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/tasks -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"title":"t","tags":[1,2,3]}' | jq .statusCode

findings.title: reorderSubtasks does not verify that subtaskIds belong to the taskId — cross-task IDOR
findings.description: The `reorderSubtasks` method verifies that the current user can read `taskId` (via `assertCanReadTask`), then issues a Prisma transaction that updates the `position` of each subtask ID in `subtaskIds` by ID only — without filtering `where: { id, taskId }`. An attacker who knows the UUID of a subtask belonging to a task they do not own can include that UUID in the `subtaskIds` array and silently reorder/mutate the `position` field of arbitrary subtasks across the application.
findings.root_cause: The WHERE clause in the subtask update only uses `{ id }` instead of `{ id, taskId }`, failing to scope updates to the parent task.
findings.impact: Any authenticated user with `tasks:update` on at least one task can silently alter the `position` field of any subtask in the database, including those belonging to tasks they cannot read. While position is a cosmetic ordering field, this represents a clear IDOR that undermines the object-ownership model.
acceptance_criteria: Attempting to reorder subtasks of another task returns HTTP 403 or the update is silently ignored (no position change on foreign subtask)
Unit test confirms that subtask.update is called with `{ id, taskId }` composite key
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: create two tasks each with one subtask, then POST /tasks/:id1/subtasks/reorder with subtaskId from task2 and verify that subtask2.position changes

findings.title: DELETE /tasks/:taskId/raci/:userId/:role — :role path param is unvalidated (no ParseEnumPipe)
findings.description: The `:role` path parameter is typed as `RACIRole` in TypeScript but extracted with a plain `@Param('role')` — no `ParseEnumPipe(RACIRole)` is applied. The global ValidationPipe does not validate path parameters; it only operates on `@Body()`. TypeScript types are erased at runtime. As a result, any arbitrary string will be accepted and passed as `role` to `removeRACI()`. In `removeRACI()` the value flows directly into a Prisma `findUnique` where clause: `taskId_userId_role: { taskId, userId, role }`. Prisma will not throw on an unknown enum value at the query level — it will simply return null and the service will throw NotFoundException. However, the lack of enum validation at the boundary is a defence-in-depth gap that could be exploited if Prisma behaviour changes or if an attacker probes for information via differential responses.
findings.root_cause: NestJS @Param decorators do not apply the global ValidationPipe; enum validation must be applied explicitly via ParseEnumPipe.
findings.impact: Calling DELETE with a fabricated role string (e.g. 'ADMIN') will not find a matching RACI row and returns 404 — leaking that the combination does not exist. Deeper impact depends on future schema changes. Currently a low-exploitation bug but a concrete validation gap.
findings.suggested_fix:
Use `new ParseEnumPipe(RACIRole)` on the param:
```ts
@Param('role', new ParseEnumPipe(RACIRole)) role: RACIRole,
```

findings.title: ImportTasksDto.tasks has no @ArrayMaxSize — unbounded bulk import enabling DoS
findings.description: The `tasks` array in `ImportTasksDto` has `@IsArray()` and `@ValidateNested()` but no `@ArrayMaxSize()`. The `importTasks` and `validateImport` service methods iterate over every element in a `for` loop with individual Prisma calls inside each iteration (including a `findFirst` duplicate check, `task.create`, and N × `subtask.create` per task). A caller with `tasks:create` can submit thousands of tasks in a single request. This will (a) consume CPU for the nested validation loop, (b) issue potentially thousands of sequential DB round-trips holding a long-lived transaction window, and (c) exhaust the body-size limit only if `rawBody` caps are low (which they may not be).
findings.root_cause: The DTO array was not bounded with @ArrayMaxSize, and the service has no limit check on tasks.length.
findings.impact: An authenticated user with `tasks:create` can trigger a slow-loris-style DB overload by submitting a single POST with thousands of task objects. Depending on server and DB configuration, this can degrade service for all users.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add `@ArrayMaxSize(500)` (or a lower business-appropriate limit) to `ImportTasksDto.tasks`. Also add a guard in the service:
```ts
if (tasks.length > 500) throw new BadRequestException('Import limited to 500 tasks per request');
```
And apply `@MaxLength()` to `ImportTaskDto.title`, `description`, `subtasks`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /tasks/project/:id/import with more than 500 tasks returns HTTP 400
POST /tasks/project/:id/import/validate with more than 500 tasks returns HTTP 400
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: craft a body with 1001 task objects and POST to the import endpoint, verify 400 is returned
findings.notes: The body-size pre-filter in main.ts (if configured) provides partial protection, but does not bound the item count independently of byte size.

findings.title: BulkAssignmentDto.userIds and BulkAssignmentDto.dates have no @ArrayMaxSize — cartesian product DoS
findings.description: The `createBulkAssignment` endpoint accepts arbitrary-length `userIds` and `dates` arrays with only a minimum size of 1. The service iterates `userIds × dates` (cartesian product) with sequential Prisma `create` calls inside the loop. A caller with `predefined_tasks:assign` can submit 1000 users × 365 dates = 365 000 DB insert attempts in one request. The same issue affects `CreateBulkRecurringRulesDto.userIds` and `daysOfWeek`.
findings.root_cause: Bulk operation DTOs were not bounded with @ArrayMaxSize on either dimension.
findings.impact: A privileged user with `predefined_tasks:assign` can trigger tens of thousands of sequential DB writes per request, causing response timeouts and potential DB connection pool exhaustion.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@ArrayMaxSize(100)` to `userIds` and `@ArrayMaxSize(365)` to `dates` in `BulkAssignmentDto`. Apply equivalent limits to `CreateBulkRecurringRulesDto.userIds` and `.daysOfWeek`. Enforce the same in the service as a defence-in-depth check.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: craft a body with large userIds/dates arrays, confirm 400 is returned after the fix
findings.notes: The daysOfWeek array in CreateBulkRecurringRulesDto is bounded at 0-6 per element but not in count; a validated array of 7 elements is the maximum semantic payload and is fine, but the userIds array is the main risk vector here.

findings.title: CreatePredefinedTaskDto free-text fields (name, description, color, icon) lack @MaxLength
findings.description: The `name`, `description`, `color`, and `icon` fields in `CreatePredefinedTaskDto` are validated with @IsString() but have no @MaxLength. The `color` field has a @Matches regex for time fields but not for color itself. `icon` (intended for emoji) is unbounded. A user with `predefined_tasks:create` can store very large strings in these fields.
findings.root_cause: MaxLength constraints were omitted during initial DTO creation.
findings.impact: Low severity for privileged-only endpoint (`predefined_tasks:create`), but unbounded DB strings are a correctness and DoS risk.
acceptance_criteria: POST /predefined-tasks with name > 200 chars returns HTTP 400

findings.title: ImportTaskDto.assigneeEmail has no @IsEmail — email validation is done by case-insensitive Map lookup instead
findings.description: The `assigneeEmail` field in `ImportTaskDto` is typed as a string with @IsString() but no @IsEmail() constraint. The import service resolves assignees by looking up the email in a pre-fetched Map (case-insensitive). While functionally this prevents invalid emails from matching, the lack of a DTO-level @IsEmail() means: (1) any string passes validation, (2) extremely long strings are not capped, and (3) the absence of a format check makes the field semantics opaque.
findings.root_cause: Email validation was deferred to the service lookup logic rather than enforced at the DTO boundary.
findings.impact: Low — the service-layer Map lookup gracefully handles non-matching strings. However, @MaxLength is still missing and a very long string will pass the DTO unimpeded.
acceptance_criteria: POST /tasks/project/:id/import with an assigneeEmail of 'not-an-email' returns HTTP 400

verified_clean: tasks/tasks.controller.ts — all controller route handlers have @RequirePermissions(…); no @Public route present; JwtAuthGuard + PermissionsGuard applied globally via APP_GUARD
tasks/tasks.service.ts — all Prisma queries use parameterized ORM calls; no $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe; no string-concatenated SQL
tasks/tasks.service.ts — no hardcoded secrets, tokens, or passwords in source
tasks/tasks.service.ts — CSV export sanitizes the filename via replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9-_]/g, '_'); Content-Disposition header uses the sanitized value
tasks/tasks.service.ts — findAll() applies accessScope.taskReadWhere(currentUser) RBAC filter; findOne() calls assertCanReadTask(); getTasksByProject() calls assertCanAccessProject()
tasks/tasks.service.ts — getTasksByAssignee() enforces tasks:readAll permission when querying another user's tasks
tasks/tasks.service.ts — reorderSubtasks() / createSubtask() / updateSubtask() / deleteSubtask() all call assertCanReadTask() before mutations
tasks/tasks.service.ts — no SSRF risk (no user-supplied URLs are fetched)
tasks/tasks.service.ts — no file upload handling
tasks/tasks.service.ts — circular dependency detection uses an explicit BFS visited-set; no injection surface

tasks/dto/create-task.dto.ts — title has @MinLength(3) + @MaxLength(200); status/priority use @IsEnum; UUIDs validated with @IsUUID; dates validated with @IsDateString; times validated with @Matches regex
tasks/dto/create-task.dto.ts — cross-field constraint ProjectRequiredWhenParentedConstraint correctly guards orphan+epic/milestone combinations
tasks/dto/update-task.dto.ts — extends PartialType; epicId/milestoneId/projectId redeclared without the cross-field check (intentional — documented in the file)
tasks/dto/assign-raci.dto.ts — userId validated with @IsUUID; role validated with @IsEnum(RACIRole)
tasks/dto/add-dependency.dto.ts — dependsOnId validated with @IsUUID + @IsNotEmpty
tasks/dto/create-subtask.dto.ts — title has @MinLength(1) + @MaxLength(500); isCompleted uses @IsBoolean; position uses @IsInt + @Min(0)
tasks/dto/reorder-subtasks.dto.ts — subtaskIds validated as @IsArray + @IsUUID('4', { each: true })
comments/comments.controller.ts — all routes have @RequirePermissions(…); CurrentUser always extracted; no @Public route
comments/comments.service.ts — update() enforces authorId === userId ownership check; remove() checks authorId or comments:delete_any/projects:manage_any/tasks:manage_any
comments/comments.service.ts — findAll() applies accessScope.taskReadWhere() filter; findOne() calls assertCanReadTask()
comments/comments.service.ts — no raw SQL; no hardcoded secrets; no SSRF
predefined-tasks/predefined-tasks.controller.ts — all route handlers have @RequirePermissions(…); JwtAuthGuard applied globally
predefined-tasks/predefined-tasks.service.ts — no raw SQL; all queries parameterized via Prisma ORM
predefined-tasks/predefined-tasks.service.ts — no hardcoded secrets or tokens
predefined-tasks/predefined-tasks.service.ts — createAssignment() checks predefined task isActive; assertTeleworkCompatibility() run before assignment creation

predefined-tasks/predefined-tasks.service.ts — removeAssignment() does findUnique before delete (existence check); no IDOR — any assignment ID can be deleted by anyone with predefined_tasks:assign (design choice)
predefined-tasks/dto/create-predefined-task.dto.ts — startTime/endTime validated with @Matches regex; weight bounded with @Min(1)/@Max(5)
predefined-tasks/dto/bulk-assignment.dto.ts — @ArrayMinSize(1) present on both userIds and dates; @IsUUID and @IsDateString validators present
predefined-tasks/dto/create-recurring-rule.dto.ts — cross-field IsValidRecurrenceConfig class-level validator ensures recurrenceType/dayOfWeek/monthlyOrdinal/monthlyDayOfMonth coherence
predefined-tasks/occurrence-generator.ts — pure function; no I/O; no injection surface; bounded by caller-provided rangeStart/rangeEnd
Global: ValidationPipe configured with whitelist:true + forbidNonWhitelisted:true + transform:true in main.ts
Global: ThrottlerBehindProxyGuard applied as APP_GUARD (global rate limiting); specific @Throttle decorators only on auth endpoints which is appropriate
Global: JwtAuthGuard applied as APP_GUARD — all endpoints require JWT by default; ForcePasswordChangeGuard also applied globally
Global: No $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe found in any file under the three audited paths
